task_completion.description: Set the location in Stellarium to the incident coordinates and altitude.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Location window is open, displaying Latitude 45° 0' 0.00" N, Longitude 40° 0' 0.00" W, and Elevation/Altitude 11000 m.

task_completion.description: Set the date and time in Stellarium to the incident time.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium Date and Time window is open, or the bottom status bar shows the date as 2023-03-05 and the time as 22:30:00.

task_completion.description: Orient the camera to the southwestern sky to locate the object.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium main view is oriented towards the Southwest (indicated by 'SW' on the horizon), with Venus visible in the sky.

task_completion.description: Capture wide-field and zoomed screenshots of the object.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Evidence of screenshots being saved, such as a 'Screenshot saved' notification in Stellarium, a terminal showing the contents of /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/ with new image files, or a file manager open to that directory showing at least two images.

task_completion.description: Create the investigation report text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like gedit, nano, or vim) is open, editing a file named 'ufo_investigation_report.txt' located in /home/ga/Desktop/.

task_completion.description: The report contains the incident details and identifies Venus.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text visible in the report includes the incident date (March 5, 2023), time (22:30), coordinates (45N, 40W), and explicitly names 'Venus' as the identified object, along with a brief explanation of why it matches the description.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Stellarium GUI to set the location, time, and view the sky.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen clicking through Stellarium menus and dialogs rather than just editing configuration files or querying an API via terminal.

integrity.description: The screenshots captured are of the actual Stellarium application showing the correct sky view.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots are genuine captures of the Stellarium window, not generic images downloaded from the internet or placeholders.

integrity.description: The report was written in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing or pasting the report contents into a text editor GUI or terminal editor, rather than bypassing the UI with a background script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct location is 45.00°N, 40.00°W at 11,000m altitude. The correct time is 2023-03-05 22:30:00 UTC. The object that matches the pilot's description in the southwestern sky at this time and location is Venus. The agent must save at least 2 screenshots to /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/ and write a report to /home/ga/Desktop/ufo_investigation_report.txt.
